title: A Quiet Place: Day One Rotten Tomatoes Scores
text: The prequel film achieved the lowest audience rating in the franchise with a 73% audience score on Rotten Tomatoes. Despite this distinction, the movie maintains a fresh designation, though it's significantly lower than previous installments. The score represents a notable decline from earlier films: Original (2018) 83% audience and 96% critical, Part II (2021) 92% audience and 91% critical, and Day One (2024) 73% audience and 84% critical. Viewers expressed disappointment that the film introduces little to no new lore about the aliens. Additional complaints centered on slow pacing and limited action sequences. However, the cast performances, particularly Lupita Nyong'o and Joseph Quinn, received praise. Despite lower ratings, Day One succeeded commercially, achieving an opening weekend franchise record with $53 million domestically and reaching $98.5 million worldwide at the time of writing.
